## This is a small header

This is basic paragraph text.

Our Conclusion: We would recommend adding prelecture videos to the course. While there was a positive correlation between students who rated the class as harder and prelecture video desire scores, there was a fairly consistent trend of all students wanting the videos (average > 3). Some potential downsides include additional workload on professors/TAs, and potential decrease in attendance if students become reliant on learning from the videos. You could collect feedback from students to see what alterations need to be made from the videos. Additionally, there could be a feature that requires students to answer questions about the content as the video goes on, which would make it so they get a lot out of the video rather than mindlessly playing it.

## Graph 1: Scatterplot ##
<img src="<custom-path>/static/imgs/graph1.pdf" alt="Graph 1"/>
We found a positive correlation between difficulty rating and desire to have prelecture videos.

## Graph 2: Bar Chart ##
<img src="<custom-path>/static/imgs/Graph2.pdf" alt="Graph 2"/>
We compared difficulty ratings and desire for prelecture videos.

## Graph 3: Line Chart ##
<img src="<custom-path>/static/imgs/graph3.pdf" alt="Graph 3"/>
We found a positive correlation between students thinking the class is difficult and wanting the videos.
